zh.lifeSummary: Jane Maxwell of Caerlaverock是Sir Herbert Maxwell of Caelavenrock和Katherine Stewart的女兒，於1397年出生在英國蘇格蘭度福利爾凱爾拉維洛可。她於1415年之前與William Douglas 2nd of Drumlanrig結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1467年6月25日在英國蘇格蘭度福利爾德盧姆藍利 卡素享年70歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭度福利爾德盧姆藍利 卡素。

nameMeanings.name: Maxwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from a place in Kelso, near Melrose in Roxburghshire, the name of a salmon pool on the Tweed near Kelso Bridge. The placename is first recorded in 1144 in the form Mackeswell ‘Mack's spring or stream’ (Old English well(a)).
Irish: adopted for Ó Meisceall, see Miskell . This surname is common in Ulster.
Jewish: arbitrary adoption of the Scottish name, or Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Miskell
meanings: Irish (Galway): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Meisceall ‘descendant of Meiscill’, a personal name of unexplained etymology. Sometimes Anglicized as Maxwell .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Jane Maxwell of Caerlaverock was born in 1397, in Caerlaverock, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom as the daughter of Sir Herbert Maxwell of Caelavenrock and Katherine Stewart. She married William Douglas 2nd of Drumlanrig before 1415. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 25 June 1467, in Drumlanrig Castle, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 70, and was buried in Drumlanrig Castle, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane Maxwell of Caerlaverock est née en 1397, à Caerlaverock, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, fille de Sir Herbert Maxwell of Caelavenrock et Katherine Stewart. Elle a épousé William Douglas 2nd of Drumlanrig avant 1415. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 25 juin 1467, à Drumlanrig Castle, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Drumlanrig Castle, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Maxwell of Caerlaverockは1397年にイギリススコットランドダムフリーシュアイアケエルラバーオックでSir Herbert Maxwell of CaelavenrockとKatherine Stewartの娘として生まれた。1415年以前にWilliam Douglas 2nd of Drumlanrigと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1467年6月25日にイギリススコットランドダムフリーシュアイアドルムランリグ カストルで70歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドダムフリーシュアイアドルムランリグ カストルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Maxwell of Caerlaverock(은)는 1397년 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리실르 카이를레이베록에서 Sir Herbert Maxwell of Caelavenrock(와)과 Katherine Stewart의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1415년 이전 William Douglas 2nd of Drumlanrig(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1467년 6월 25일 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리실르 드럼랜리그 카스틀에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리실르 드럼랜리그 카스틀에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Jane Maxwell of Caerlaverock родилась в 1397 г. в местности каирлавирокк, думфриессхир, Шотландия, Великобритания. Её родителей звали Sir Herbert Maxwell of Caelavenrock и Katherine Stewart. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Douglas 2nd of Drumlanrig перед 1415 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 25 июня 1467 г. в местности друмланриг кастл, думфриессхир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности друмланриг кастл, думфриессхир, Шотландия, Великобритания.
